           DAN BECK and BIENVENIDA SALAZAR,    CASE NO.: CGC-21-589177              
       11  individually and on behalf of all others similarly                       
           situated,                           DECLARATION  OF JULIAN HAMMOND       
       12                                      IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS’ MOTION     
                   Plaintiffs,                 FOR DISMISSAL OF CLASS ACTION        
       13                                      COMPLAINT  WITHOUT PREJUDICE         
                                               PURSUANT TO CRC 3.770                
           vs.                                                                      
       14                                                                           
           UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, a                                           
       15  California Corporation,                                                  
       16          Defendant.

    29                                                                              
                    DECLARATION OF JULIAN HAMMOND ISO PLAINTIFFS’ REQUEST TO DISMISS CLASS ACTION
    30                                                                              
                                                                                    
    31                                                                              
 
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                I, Julian Hammond, based on my personal knowledge, declare and state as follows:
        1                                                                           
                1.   I am an attorney licensed, authorized, and admitted to practice law before this Court and
        2                                                                           
           the federal and state courts of the State of California. I am a founding shareholder in the law firm of
        3                                                                           
           HammondLaw, P.C., and counsel of record for Plaintiffs Dan Beck and Bienvenida Salazar (“Plaintiffs”)
        4  in this action.                                                          
        5       2.   This lawsuit, filed on January 19, 2021, is one of three identical wage and hour class
           action lawsuits against University of San Francisco pending in the San Francisco Superior Court. The
        6                                                                           
           other two actions are: Dan Beck et al. v. University of San Francisco, Case No. CGC-21-588930, filed
        7                                                                           
           January 5, 2021 and attached as Exhibit 1; and Dan Beck et al. v. University of San Francisco, Case No.
        8                                                                           
           CGC-21-589038, filed January 12, 2021, and attached as Exhibit 2.        
        9                                                                           
                3.   The three identical lawsuits were each brought by Plaintiffs pursuant to California Code
       10                                                                           
           of Civil Procedure § 382, on behalf of themselves and all other similarly situated employees of Defendant
       11  University of San Francisco. The Complaints in each of the lawsuits identically seek damages for unpaid
       12  wages, unpaid premium pay for missed meal and rest breaks, unreimbursed expenses, restitution,
           statutory penalties, interest, injunctive and equitable relief, and reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs
       13                                                                           
           pursuant to California Labor Code §§ 218.5, 226(a), (e), 226.7, 510, 512, 1194, 2802, Wage Order No.
       14                                                                           
           4-2001, §§ 3, 11, and 12, California Code of Civil Procedure § 1021.5, and California Business &
       15                                                                           
           Professions Code §§ 17200 et seq.                                        
       16                                                                           
                4.   This lawsuit and the January 12 lawsuit were filed only after my office was erroneously
       17  advised that Plaintiffs’ first-filed lawsuit (Case No. CGC-21-588930) had been rejected by the Civil
       18  Clerk and had not been filed.                                            
                5.   Neither this lawsuit nor the January 12 lawsuit have been served on the Defendant.
       19                                                                           
                6.   Plaintiffs are only seeking to dismiss this lawsuit and (by separate request filed
       20                                                                           
           concurrently herewith) the January 12 lawsuit.                           
       21                                                                           
                7.   The first-filed lawsuit, Case No. CGC-21-588930, has been served on the Defendant.
       22                                                                           
           Plaintiffs are not seeking to dismiss their first-filed action and are litigating that action.
       23       8.   No class certification motion has been filed in this case, no class has been certified, and
       24  notice of the pendency of this action has not been provided, nor is required to be provided, to any class
           members. The class claims will be dismissed without prejudice. Plaintiffs’ counsel is unaware of any
       25                                                                           
           class members who have relied on this action or refrained from bringing similar claims due to the
       26                                                                           
           existence of this action.                                                
       27                                                                           
                9.   Neither Plaintiffs nor Plaintiffs’ counsel are receiving any consideration, direct or
       28                                                                           
                                             1                                      
    29                                                                              
                    DECLARATION OF JULIAN HAMMOND ISO PLAINTIFFS’ REQUEST TO DISMISS CLASS ACTION
    30                                                                              
    31                                                                              
 
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
           indirect, for this dismissal.                                            
        1                                                                           
                10.  Dismissal of this action is appropriate without notice to class members because (a) class
        2                                                                           
           members will not suffer any prejudice as result of the dismissal because the class claims will be
        3                                                                           
           dismissed without prejudice; (b) neither Plaintiffs nor Plaintiffs’ counsel are receiving any
        4  consideration for their request; and (c) class members will have a full and fair opportunity to pursue
        5  their claims in the first-filed lawsuit, i.e., Case No. CGC-21-588930.   
                I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the foregoing is
        6                                                                           
           true and correct. Executed on March 23, 2021.                            
        7                                                                           
        8                                                                           
                Julian Hammond
